committee.name: Operations Manager
interests: Whether the wizard can be handed off to a hiring manager without needing a lawyer on standby for every new hire
How long it actually takes to walk through the 45-step wizard start to finish, especially during a busy hiring period
Whether the generated agreement can be saved, re-opened, and edited without starting over from step one
Whether role selection — Employer vs. Employee — correctly pre-fills or skips sections relevant to a veterinary practice context
How the wizard handles required fields that a manager doesn't know off the top of their head, like a specific pay date structure
Whether the finished agreement is print-ready or needs reformatting before we can hand it to a new hire
How we track which agreements have been generated and whether they've been signed
Whether the wizard captures enough detail that the final agreement holds up if a hire doesn't work out
How often the underlying model agreement gets updated and whether we'll be notified when we need to regenerate
Whether we can use the same wizard for different roles — a technician vs. a receptionist — without getting confused by terms that don't apply
decisionCriteria: A non-lawyer manager can complete the wizard and produce a usable agreement without external help
The wizard finishes in a reasonable sitting — under 30 minutes — for a typical veterinary practice hire
Required fields are clearly marked and the agreement won't generate until everything is filled in, preventing incomplete contracts
The Employer role path covers the terms we actually need — compensation, schedule, probationary period, duties
We can save progress mid-wizard and return without losing answers already entered
The generated agreement looks professional enough to hand directly to a new hire without reformatting
There's a way to know which agreements are outstanding vs. completed without digging through email
The wizard reflects veterinary-specific language and environment, not a generic office-job template
Updates to the underlying model are clearly communicated so we don't unknowingly hand out a stale agreement
We can run through the wizard more than once for different hires without each session bleeding into the next
goals: Get from 'we need to hire someone' to a signed agreement faster than the current patchwork of Word templates and lawyer reviews
Eliminate the version-confusion problem where different managers use different versions of the employment agreement
Free up the practice owner's time by letting the operations team handle agreement generation independently
Reduce the risk of a legally incomplete employment agreement slipping through because a required field was skipped
Build a repeatable hiring process where employment agreements are consistent from one hire to the next
Stop paying for one-off legal reviews every time a standard agreement is needed for a routine position
Have a clear record of what terms were offered to each employee, in case of a dispute
Make the onboarding handoff smoother by producing agreements that employees can actually read and understand
Ensure the veterinary practice environment settings in the wizard match how our practice actually operates
Reduce the back-and-forth between the office and the practice owner every time a new agreement needs to be generated
objections: If the wizard is 45 steps, managers will start it and then abandon it halfway through when they get pulled into something urgent — is there a save-and-resume feature?
We already have Word templates that work fine; convincing everyone to switch to a new wizard is a change management problem
I'm not sure a model agreement built for veterinary practices will match the specific terms we've negotiated with staff over the years
If this is a public VIN Foundation tool, are we actually in control of what terms are included, or do we have to take whatever the model says?
The wizard generates the agreement but doesn't handle signing — so we're still managing a separate step for that
If the underlying model agreement gets updated, every manager needs to know they should be using the new version, not the one they printed last month
What happens when a manager fills in the wizard but the owner disagrees with what was offered — can it be corrected before it goes to the employee?
For part-time or relief staff, some of these 45 steps may not apply — will the wizard make that clear or will it force us through irrelevant sections?
We'd need to explain this tool to every manager who might do hiring — that's training time we don't always have
If the agreement is only as good as what the manager enters, we're still exposed if someone answers a required field incorrectly
openQuestions: Can I save my progress in the wizard and come back to it later, or do I have to finish in one sitting?
When I select 'Employer' as my role, which sections are pre-populated vs. which ones do I have to fill in manually?
How does the wizard handle a step where I'm not sure of the correct answer — can I flag it and come back?
Once the agreement is generated, can I make edits to specific clauses without re-running the whole wizard?
Does the wizard produce a clean, formatted document I can email directly to a new hire, or do I need to do formatting work first?
Is there a way to see a summary or preview of the agreement before I finalize it?
If the underlying model agreement changes, will agreements I've already generated be flagged as outdated?
Can I run the wizard for a technician role and a receptionist role and get appropriately different outputs?
What happens if I skip a required field — does the wizard block me or warn me at the end?
Is there any kind of audit trail showing who generated which agreement and when?
How do I know which version of the model agreement I'm working from?
Is the veterinary-environment setting just a label, or does it actually change the language and terms in the agreement?
Can the same wizard be used across multiple locations of the same practice, or is it tied to one setup?
If a manager makes a mistake in step 20 of 45, can they go back without having to redo everything that came after?
Is there a way to compare the agreement I just generated against one from six months ago to see what changed?

committee.name: Administration Manager
interests: How the wizard organizes agreement data so the front office can retrieve a copy of any employee's agreement without hunting through a shared drive
Whether completed agreements can be stored and accessed within the system or just downloaded and filed elsewhere
How the wizard's required-field logic prevents an incomplete agreement from being printed and handed to a new hire in error
Whether the tool supports multiple staff members running separate wizard sessions simultaneously without interfering with each other
How much administrative overhead is added by using the wizard versus the current template process
Whether the system gives any notification when a model agreement update means previously generated agreements are now outdated
How the tool handles corrections — if a wrong answer was entered, what does fixing it look like in practice
Whether the generated agreement includes all the statutory language required for an employment agreement in a veterinary practice context
How the wizard handles the difference between full-time, part-time, and relief veterinary staff in terms of which sections apply
Whether the practice can maintain a record of all agreements generated without building a separate tracking spreadsheet
decisionCriteria: The administrative workload per hire doesn't increase — the wizard should replace steps, not add them
Completed agreements are consistently formatted so filing and retrieval are straightforward
The required-field completion gate means no agreement can be generated with blanks in legally important places
Non-technical admin staff can operate the wizard correctly after a single walkthrough with no specialized knowledge
The tool either stores generated agreements accessibly or exports them in a format that fits our existing filing system
There is a clear way to know when an agreement was generated and under which version of the model
Corrections to a wizard session are possible without generating a second conflicting agreement by accident
The wizard's veterinary-environment framing eliminates sections that are clearly irrelevant to a practice office context
Support or documentation is available when the front office hits an unfamiliar step in the wizard
The tool works reliably — a wizard session shouldn't freeze or lose data mid-way through
goals: Eliminate the situation where two managers have printed different versions of the employment agreement for the same position
Give the front office a clear, predictable process for producing an employment agreement every time a hire is made
Reduce reliance on the practice owner to review and approve every generated agreement for routine positions
Have a retrievable record for every agreement generated, indexed to the employee and the date
Stop spending time formatting Word templates before they're usable — get something print-ready directly from the wizard
Make the agreement generation step something any office manager can own without legal training
Ensure new agreements reflect the current model, not a template someone saved to their desktop two years ago
Create consistency in the employment terms being offered so there are no disparities between staff doing similar jobs
Reduce the time from 'offer accepted' to 'agreement signed' by shortening the agreement preparation step
Have a defensible record of what was agreed to at hire in case of a dispute or departure
objections: If agreements are just downloaded files and not stored in the system, we've just swapped one filing problem for another
Training every front office manager to use a 45-step wizard correctly takes time we don't have during a busy hiring stretch
The wizard's model terms may not match the specific language the practice owner has always used in their agreements
If the wizard doesn't integrate with our HR or payroll system, we're still entering the same data in two places
What happens if the wizard is down or unavailable when we need to generate an agreement urgently?
A public tool from VIN Foundation is useful but I'm not sure it covers jurisdiction-specific requirements that matter for our state
If a correction is made after the agreement is generated, there's no clear process for ensuring the employee received the corrected version
The wizard generates the agreement — but signing, filing, and reminders are still outside the tool, so we haven't fully solved the workflow
For practices with multiple locations, there's no obvious way to distinguish which location's agreement is which
If VIN Foundation updates the model agreement, there's no automatic way to know which employees' agreements are now out of date
openQuestions: Where does the generated agreement actually live after I download it — is there a record inside the wizard system, or just the file I saved locally?
Can I look up whether an agreement was generated for a specific employee and when, without digging through a file folder?
What happens to my wizard session if my browser closes mid-way through — do I lose everything?
Is there any version label on the generated agreement so I know which model it was built from?
Can I run a new wizard session for the same position and compare it to the one I generated last month to see if terms have changed?
How does the wizard handle a scenario where our practice offers terms that differ from the model defaults?
Can the front office manager and the practice owner both access the same wizard session at different times, or is it single-user?
If I realize I made an error in step 15 after I've finished the wizard, what's the correction process?
Does the finished agreement include a version stamp or generation date so I know exactly what the employee was handed?
Is there a way to export a list of all agreements generated over the past year for an audit or records review?
Does the veterinary-environment setting affect the agreement's boilerplate language, or is it just used for routing the wizard path?
Can the wizard distinguish between a full-time associate veterinarian and a part-time relief tech, producing appropriately scoped agreements?
How do I handle a required field where our practice has a non-standard policy — can I override the model's default?
If VIN Foundation retires or updates the model agreement, will we be notified before we accidentally generate an outdated one?
Is there a preview mode where I can review the full generated agreement before it's finalized, without it being treated as complete?

committee.name: Procurement Manager
interests: Understanding the total cost of using modelcontract.software versus the current process of legal template reviews per hire
Whether this is a standalone subscription, a VIN Foundation membership benefit, or a per-use tool — and what triggers a cost
How the tool compares to other contract template services the practice has considered or trialed
Whether there are hidden costs downstream — like needing a lawyer to review the output before it's used
How the pricing model scales if the practice grows and generates more agreements per year
Whether the VIN Foundation branding and public nature of the wizard affects its credibility with new hires reviewing the agreement
Whether this tool can replace any existing subscription the practice is already paying for
How much staff time is actually saved per hire compared to the current process
Whether the practice would be locked into this tool or could switch without losing agreement history
What the cancellation or non-renewal process looks like if the practice decides to stop using it
decisionCriteria: The total cost — subscription plus any downstream legal review — is lower than the current cost per agreement
Pricing is transparent: flat, per-use, or clearly tied to a VIN membership benefit with no surprises
There are no significant switching costs — if the practice stops using it, existing agreements remain accessible
The wizard produces output credible enough that a lawyer review is optional rather than mandatory
The tool replaces at least one other cost the practice is currently bearing, rather than just adding to the stack
No multi-year commitment is required before the practice has validated it works for their specific needs
The value case is clear to the practice owner without requiring a detailed ROI analysis
The tool is maintained and updated, so the model agreement stays current without additional cost to the practice
Any overage or volume pricing is predictable, not exposure to surprise invoices mid-year
The practice retains ownership of all generated agreements and data regardless of subscription status
goals: Confirm the practice isn't paying for a redundant tool that duplicates something already covered by the VIN membership
Make sure the total cost of generating employment agreements per year goes down, not up, after adopting the wizard
Avoid committing the practice to a paid subscription before the team has actually tested the wizard on a real hire
Understand what happens to generated agreements if the practice's VIN membership lapses or they stop using the tool
Make a clear recommendation to the practice owner on whether this is a better value than ad-hoc legal template reviews
Identify whether there are cost-effective ways to use the wizard more — like for supplier or contractor agreements — not just employment
Ensure the practice isn't exposed to liability if the model agreement turns out to be incomplete for their jurisdiction
Avoid a situation where the tool is adopted, then a legal review reveals the agreements need to be redone anyway
Get clarity on any volume discounts if the practice has multiple locations or a high annual hiring volume
Document the cost comparison clearly enough that the practice owner can approve without a lengthy back-and-forth
objections: If this is a free VIN Foundation public tool, why are we evaluating it as a procurement decision — what's the actual cost exposure?
The wizard builds employment agreements, but we still need a lawyer to verify the output is legally sufficient for our state — that cost isn't eliminated
If the model agreement is publicly available, our competitors are using the same template, which may not reflect our specific employment terms
I'd want to know whether VIN Foundation guarantees the model stays current with employment law changes, or whether that's on us to monitor
A practice our size doesn't generate that many employment agreements per year — the time savings may not justify the adoption effort
If the wizard doesn't integrate with our HR or payroll setup, the data still has to be entered twice, which undercuts the efficiency argument
We'd need to understand whether staff training time is a real cost here — 45 steps is not trivial to learn while handling other duties
If VIN Foundation sunsets this tool or changes the model significantly, we'd need a plan for managing agreements already in circulation
The public nature of the tool raises a question about whether new hires will perceive the agreement as personalized or as a generic download
Before committing, I'd want a side-by-side of what this costs against what we're currently spending on agreement preparation
openQuestions: Is modelcontract.software included in a VIN membership, or is it a separate subscription with its own cost?
What's the per-agreement cost, if any, and does it change with volume?
If we stop using the tool, do we retain access to download agreements we've already generated, or do they become inaccessible?
Does VIN Foundation update the model agreement when employment law changes, and is that included in the base cost?
Is there a free trial period where we can run real wizard sessions before committing to any paid tier?
How does this tool's cost compare to a per-hire legal template review from an employment attorney?
Are there any add-ons or upsells — like a signing feature or a storage service — that would increase the total cost over time?
What's the minimum commitment — is there a month-to-month option, or is the first contract a full year?
If we have two locations generating agreements independently, are they billed separately or under one account?
Does the wizard support non-employment agreements — contractor or supplier terms — or is it strictly employment-focused?
What happens to our data if VIN Foundation discontinues the wizard product?
Is there a published roadmap showing whether the tool will expand beyond employment agreements?
Can I get a cost summary report showing how many agreements have been generated and what the equivalent legal-review cost would have been?
Are there any compliance certifications or guarantees attached to the model agreement — or is it explicitly provided as a starting point only?
If we outgrow the current wizard and need custom terms, is there a paid tier that supports that, or would we need to move to a different product?

committee.name: Contract Administrator
interests: Whether the wizard's 45-step flow covers every material term she's responsible for tracking — compensation, duties, probationary period, non-compete, termination — without omissions
How the required-field completion gate protects against agreements going out with blanks in critical clauses
Whether the Employer path in the wizard reflects how the practice actually structures its agreements, not just a generic template
How she can tell, at a glance, that an agreement generated today was built from the current version of the model, not an outdated one
Whether the wizard can surface a summary of key agreed terms — start date, compensation, role, schedule — without making her read the full document every time
How the tool handles amendments when terms need to change after the original agreement is already signed
Whether there is an audit trail showing who generated each agreement, when, and which wizard answers were given
How the tool handles the veterinary-environment framing — whether it actually changes contract language or just labels the session
Whether she can flag a specific wizard step as 'needs owner review' before the agreement is finalized
Whether the wizard's output is self-consistent — no contradictions between clauses introduced by different step answers
How to track which employees are working under agreements generated in the current model version versus a prior one
Whether the wizard supports re-running a session for the same employee if terms are renegotiated
How the tool communicates model-agreement updates — email, dashboard notification, or nothing at all
Whether generated agreements can be annotated or marked with internal notes that don't appear in the employee-facing copy
How the wizard handles jurisdictional requirements — whether it flags steps that have state-specific implications
decisionCriteria: The wizard's completed output requires no manual corrections before it can be sent to a new hire — what comes out is what goes out
Every required employment term is covered by a wizard step, with no significant gap that would require supplementing the agreement by hand
The required-field gate is enforced — she can verify that no agreement left the wizard with an unanswered material step
The veterinary-environment setting changes actual contract language, not just the session label
There is a retrievable record of every wizard session including the answers given, so she can reconstruct why specific terms were included
The model version is stamped on every generated agreement so she can confirm currency without guessing
The tool either prevents or clearly flags contradictions between answers given at different steps
She can tell the practice owner which employees are on a current-model agreement and which are not, at any time
Re-running the wizard for an amended agreement produces a clean new document, not a patched version of the old one
The time to produce a complete, error-free agreement is meaningfully shorter than the current template-plus-review process
goals: Own the full employment agreement lifecycle for the practice — from wizard generation to signing to version tracking — without needing legal support for routine hires
Eliminate the risk of an employee receiving an agreement with a missing or ambiguous clause because a required field was left blank
Build a single source of truth for all employment agreements — knowing exactly what was agreed to, when, and under which model version
Flag every existing employee whose agreement was generated under an outdated model, so the practice can decide whether to update
Reduce the turnaround time from offer acceptance to signed agreement, removing the bottleneck of template preparation
Create a consistent, repeatable agreement process that doesn't depend on which manager is handling the hire
Ensure the practice can produce a defensible record of employment terms for any employee, on demand, without searching through archived files
Stop the pattern of agreements being modified ad-hoc by managers after generation, producing inconsistent and untracked variants
Be the go-to person for employment agreement questions because she controls the wizard process and the version history
Make the agreement generation step reliable enough that the practice owner no longer needs to be involved for standard positions
objections: If the wizard doesn't produce a truly complete agreement — if I still have to add clauses manually — then I've just traded one template problem for another
I need to be able to see what terms are in the agreement before it's finalized, not just after — is there a full preview before the generation step?
There's no clear mechanism in the wizard for flagging a step that needs owner approval before I proceed — I'd want that checkpoint built in
If someone re-runs the wizard for an employee and generates a second agreement, there's a risk we end up with two conflicting documents for the same person
The 45 steps assume I know the right answers — for a new position or unusual arrangement, I may need guidance on what to enter, and the wizard may not provide it
If the model agreement is updated mid-year, I need a clear notification, not just a version change that I discover by accident
I can't tell from the outside whether the veterinary-environment setting materially changes the agreement or just routes the wizard differently
The wizard generates the agreement but doesn't handle signing or storage — so the administrative steps after generation are still manual and untracked
If a new hire and I are reviewing the agreement together and they spot an issue, correcting it after generation is unclear — do I re-run the whole wizard?
For practices with relief staff or contractors, the employment wizard may not produce the right document type — is there a different wizard path for non-employee agreements?
openQuestions: Can I see a full preview of the generated agreement before I click 'generate' — so I can catch issues before the document is finalized?
Is there a step in the wizard where I can mark that the practice owner needs to review this session before it proceeds?
How does the wizard handle a scenario where two different sessions are run for the same employee — does it warn me, or do I end up with two separate agreements?
What happens if I answer step 32 in a way that contradicts what I entered at step 12 — does the wizard catch that?
Is there a version stamp on the generated agreement so I can tell at a glance whether it was built from the current model or an older one?
Can I produce a list of all employees sorted by which model-agreement version their contract was generated under?
If VIN Foundation updates the model agreement, how am I notified — email, a banner in the wizard, or nothing?
Does the veterinary-environment setting actually change the text of the agreement, or just the wizard path?
Can I annotate the wizard session with internal notes that are saved in the system but not included in the employee-facing document?
If a required field is jurisdiction-specific — like a non-compete clause — does the wizard flag that, or does it just include the model default without warning?
What does the amendment process look like — if terms change six months after the original agreement, do I re-run the wizard from scratch?
Can I download a structured summary of the key agreed terms — compensation, role, start date, schedule — separately from the full agreement text?
Is there a way to lock a generated agreement so no one can re-run the wizard and accidentally produce a superseding version without my knowing?
What's the completeness check — can I verify that every required step was answered before the agreement was generated, after the fact?
If a clause in the generated agreement is later found to be missing something legally important, is there any support from VIN Foundation, or is the model agreement provided 'as is'?
Can I pull up the exact answers that were entered in the wizard for a specific employee's agreement, a year after it was generated?

committee.name: Compliance Manager
interests: Whether the model agreement produced by the wizard meets minimum legal requirements for an employment agreement in the relevant jurisdiction, not just as a starting point
How VIN Foundation maintains the model agreement and whether there is a documented process for updating it when employment law changes
Whether the wizard's required-field gate ensures all legally material terms are captured before an agreement is generated
How the practice can demonstrate, in an audit or dispute, that a specific agreement was generated using a specific model version on a specific date
Whether the veterinary-environment setting produces language that is appropriate for veterinary practice employment, including regulated-profession terms
How the tool handles jurisdiction-specific variations — whether it flags state-specific requirements or applies a generic model that may miss local law
Whether the audit trail produced by the wizard captures enough detail to reconstruct the agreement terms if the original document is lost or disputed
How the practice manages a situation where an outdated agreement is still in force for an employee whose model version has since been superseded
Whether there are any disclaimers or limitations on the VIN Foundation model agreement that the practice should understand before relying on it
How the wizard handles regulated terms — like required notice periods or statutory minimum provisions — that can't be waived by the employer
decisionCriteria: The model agreement is clearly positioned as a legal starting point with appropriate disclaimers — not as a fully validated legal instrument for all jurisdictions
There is a documented update cadence for the model agreement so the practice knows how frequently it is reviewed for legal currency
The wizard's audit trail is sufficient to reconstruct what was agreed to and when, for any generated agreement, in a dispute or review context
The veterinary-environment setting produces materially different and appropriate language, not just a label applied to a generic employment template
The required-field completion gate prevents generation of agreements with missing legally required terms
The practice can produce a version history showing which model was in use when any specific agreement was generated
There is a notification mechanism when the model is updated, so the practice can identify employees whose agreements need to be reviewed
The wizard does not allow an employer to waive a statutory minimum term — either the field is locked or a warning is surfaced
The tool's data handling is appropriate — generated agreements and wizard session data are not stored or accessible by unauthorized parties
The practice can determine, without extensive manual research, whether a generated agreement is likely sufficient for their state's employment law requirements
goals: Ensure the practice is not unknowingly issuing employment agreements that are missing legally required terms or contain unenforceable clauses
Build a reliable process for identifying when any employee's agreement was generated under an outdated model version, so remediation can be prioritized
Create a defensible paper trail for every employment agreement — generation date, model version, required fields completed — in case of a dispute or regulatory review
Understand exactly what VIN Foundation does and does not guarantee about the model agreement, so the practice knows what additional review is needed
Reduce the compliance exposure created by managers using informal or inconsistent agreement templates without oversight
Establish a clear process for handling model-agreement updates — who is notified, what the review trigger is, and which existing agreements need to be revisited
Make sure the wizard's veterinary-environment framing reflects regulated-profession requirements relevant to veterinary employment, not just general language
Ensure the practice can produce a complete agreement history on demand — for an employment tribunal, a records request, or an internal audit
Eliminate the situation where an employment dispute surfaces a clause that was never in the model — added ad-hoc by a manager after generation
Give the practice owner confidence that the agreement generation process, while not a replacement for legal counsel, is not a compliance liability on its own
objections: A model agreement from a professional foundation is a useful starting point, but it's not a legal guarantee — the practice may still need a jurisdiction-specific review before relying on it
If VIN Foundation doesn't publish a clear update history for the model agreement, the practice has no way to know when a review is overdue
The wizard generates agreements but doesn't track signing — so the compliance record is incomplete without a separate signing and storage process
Jurisdiction-specific requirements — like state-mandated notice periods or non-compete limits — may not be flagged by the wizard, leaving a gap the practice has to fill
If a manager enters a non-compliant answer in the wizard — like a probationary period that exceeds legal limits — does the wizard catch it, or does it just generate the agreement?
The audit trail from the wizard may not be sufficient for a formal dispute if it only records that the agreement was generated, not the specific answers entered at each step
Practices in states with frequent employment law updates may find the model agreement lags behind current requirements between VIN Foundation's review cycles
The public availability of the tool means the practice can't claim the model agreement is proprietary — any departing employee could compare their agreement against the model
If the wizard is updated and the model changes, agreements in force under the old model aren't automatically flagged — the practice has to manage that manually
For regulated veterinary roles — licensed veterinarians, credentialed technicians — the model agreement may not address profession-specific obligations or licensing terms
openQuestions: Does VIN Foundation publish a documented review cycle for the model agreement, and how frequently is it updated for legal currency?
What disclaimers are attached to the model agreement — is it presented as jurisdiction-specific legal advice or as a general starting point?
Does the wizard flag steps where a state-specific requirement may differ from the model default — for example, non-compete enforceability limits?
What does the audit trail include — just the generation date and model version, or the actual answers entered at each wizard step?
Can I pull an audit report showing all agreements generated in the past year, with their model version and generation date, for a compliance review?
If a manager enters a term that likely violates a statutory minimum — like an illegal probationary period — does the wizard warn them or block them?
How do I know if an existing employee's agreement was generated under a model version that has since been updated in a legally material way?
For a licensed veterinarian's employment agreement, does the veterinary-environment setting include profession-specific terms relevant to their licensure obligations?
What happens to the wizard session data after the agreement is generated — is it retained, for how long, and who can access it?
Does VIN Foundation notify practices when a model update involves a change to a legally required term, as distinct from a minor language revision?
Is there a way to run the wizard in a 'review mode' where I can see which terms are model defaults versus which are configurable without actually generating an agreement?
Can I compare the current model agreement against the version in use when a specific employee's agreement was generated, to identify material differences?
How does the wizard handle a required field where the employer's policy is non-compliant — does it block, warn, or just generate the agreement as entered?
What is the process if a generated agreement is later found to contain an error that creates a compliance exposure — is there any recourse with VIN Foundation?
For a practice with staff in multiple states, does the wizard support jurisdiction-specific sessions, or does it apply a single model regardless of employee location?
Is there any third-party legal review process that VIN Foundation uses to validate the model agreement before publishing it?
Can I set up an internal notification so our office is alerted whenever a new wizard session is initiated, so I can confirm compliance before the agreement goes to the employee?
